name: Chung-Hsin Electric and Machinery Manufacturing Corp.
summary: Chung-Hsin Electric and Machinery Manufacturing Corp. manufactures and sells electric motors and generators in Taiwan and internationally. It operates through Electricity power, Engineering, and Service segments. It offers gas insulated switchgears, gas circuit breakers, gas insulated transmission lines and combined switchgears, fuse cutouts, load break switchgears, and low voltage switchgears; electro-mechanical, electronic single phase, and polyphase meters; and equipment for the monitoring and control of the electric facilities at power plants, wind farms, transformer stations, and distribution feeders. The company also provides precision air-conditionings for computer rooms, blowers for chiller systems, and chiller machines; alternators and diesel-electric engine sets; diesel generators; backup power fuel, cell systems; micro-grid systems; machine tool products; and precision machines for fabrication and welding, sheet metal works, surface treating, and inspection and test. In addition, it offers GIS/GCB welding tanks and enclosures, GIS/GCB aluminum casting tanks and enclosures, GIL enclosures, GIS/GCB aluminum conductors, stainless steel and aluminum vacuum chambers, and machining services and customer made parts; gas insulated transformer neutral grounding of disconnecting switch systems; DC power switchgears and neutral grounding resistors for transformers; and casting aluminum alloy conductors and pressure vessels for high-voltage GCB and ABS. Further, the company undertakes contracts for power generation and substation projects; constructs automatic parking facilities; and sells and rents residence and building properties. Additionally, it provides engineering, urban regeneration, energy management, and parking management services. The company was formerly known as Chung-Hsin Electric Factory and changed its name to Chung-Hsin Electric and Machinery Manufacturing Corp. in 1962. The company was founded in 1956 and is headquartered in New Taipei City, Taiwan.
